items.title: [RISK-ON] Progress toward a US-Iran ceasefire dominates the weekend tape, fueling a rec…
items.content_text:
Progress toward a US-Iran ceasefire dominates the weekend tape, fueling a record-high equity run and cooling safe-haven USD demand. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1659; easing geopolitical friction and a +169bp OIS carry advantage for USD is likely to keep the pair capped at the open. USD/JPY closed Friday at 159.25; while risk-on sentiment would typically support the pair, fading Fed hike bets and a strong JPY CESI (+12) set up downside risk for the Tokyo open. BoC's June 10 meeting is the next key focus.

Key signals:
• NZD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical De-escalation: NZD outperformance (1W avg +1.64%) combined with JPY weakness (-0.66%) creates a 2.30% G8 spread that is likely to widen as US-Iran ceasefir
• AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind Risk vs Carry Support: AUD COT net long +57.1k (74.5% long) creates a crowded-long unwind risk despite the 73bp OIS carry advantage favoring the AUD. While US-Iran
• USD/JPY — Yield Gap vs Squeeze Risk: USD/JPY closed Friday at 159.25, supported by a 289bp OIS carry advantage and a bull steepener regime in the US curve (+46.3bp 2s10s). Howev
